title: Progressive disclosure /report — 3-tier CEO dashboard
description: The /report skill currently dumps everything at one level. Restructure into 3 tiers: (1) Headline — 5-line status (what shipped, what's blocked, what needs CEO), (2) Summary — per-goal progress and recommendations (current daily mode), (3) Deep dive — full initiative details and agent performance (current weekly mode). Headline is the default. CEO drills down with /report summary or /report deep.

in: Restructure /report SKILL.md into 3 tiers: headline (default), summary, deep
Headline tier: 5 lines max — shipped count, blocked count, needs-CEO count, top recommendation, health status
Summary tier: current daily mode output
Deep tier: current weekly mode output
CLI argument parsing: /report (headline), /report summary, /report deep, /report weekly (alias for deep)

dod.criterion: /report with no args produces a 5-line headline
Headline tier is readable and actionable in <10 seconds

checklist: Run /report — confirm headline output is <6 lines
Run /report summary — confirm it matches current daily mode
Run /report deep — confirm it matches current weekly mode
Have Marcus review headline tier for CEO-readability

reviewers.domain: product — is the headline useful? Does progressive disclosure feel natural?
process — do tiers contain the right information at the right level?

tasks.title: Design 3-tier structure — what goes in each tier, argument parsing
Build headline tier — extract top-level signals from existing report data
Refactor /report SKILL.md — add argument routing, map daily→summary and weekly→deep
Test all three tiers and verify backward compatibility with /report daily and /report weekly
